what: max-tractable z_0(λ,a) roughness closure + log-law sensitivity + one-field-point flag

settled_legs.amplitude: MEASURED amplitude-independent (§G.6/figures/59) -> z_0 is geometry-set, not steepness-set

one_field_point.needed: a single scallop train with BOTH geometry (λ,a) AND an independent drag measure (near-wall velocity profile -> z_0, or measured u* + dφ/ds) on the same train
one_field_point.pins: the Nikuradse geometry prefactor c_z=α_s/30 (amplitude leg already settled)
one_field_point.candidates: Curl 1966 / Blumberg & Curl 1974 (limestone & ice scallops); cave-scallop morphometry; subglacial-conduit dye-trace + pressure

verdict: MAX-TRACTABLE THEORY: with wavelength (Curl) and amplitude (§G.6 measured) legs settled, only the Nikuradse geometry prefactor c_z∈[0.01,0.1] (~10× uncertain) is open. At the Curl anchor (u*=0.05 -> λ≈7.9 cm, a≈7.9 mm, H=2.4 m), z_0∈[7.9e-05,7.9e-04] m gives C_d∈[1.6e-03,2.6e-03] — the 10× roughness uncertainty BUFFERS to only ~1.7× in C_d (and dφ/ds), because the log law depends only on ln(H/z_0) with H/z_0~10³-10⁴. So the missing field point is the framework's least damaging open closure; the single (geometry+drag) scallop-train point that would pin c_z is specified, and does not exist in-repo (no faked field verification).
references: this repo §A.2/§G.6 (figures/59); Curl 1966; Blumberg & Curl 1974; Nikuradse 1933; Röthlisberger 1972; Werder et al. 2013
